data.title: Starwood Real Estate Income Trust temporarily suspends redemptions
data.description:
Starwood Real Estate Income Trust has temporarily suspended redemptions, including share repurchase requests submitted for April, effective Wednesday, the company said in a statement.

"We are continuing to actively explore strategic capital raises that would support liquidity and potentially drive additional reliable NAV per share growth through new investments in other real estate sectors," Starwood Capital's CEO Barry Sternlicht said in a letter to shareholders.

data.title: Core Laboratories Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Core Laboratories Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 6 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 14 cents. The mean expectation of three analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 11 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 6 cents to 21 cents per share.

  • Revenue fell 1.4% to $121.80 million from a year ago; analysts expected $124.94 million.

  • Core Laboratories Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of 2 cents​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $789 million.

  • Core Laboratories Inc shares had fallen by 0.1% this quarter and gained 4.6% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 22.1% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, one analyst negatively revised an earnings estimate

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 1 "strong buy" or "buy," 1 "hold" and 1 "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the business support services peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Core Laboratories Inc is $15.00, about 15.3% below its last closing price of $17.30

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data April 29 at 11:21 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Australia's Woolworths beats third-quarter sales expectations
data.description:
Australia's Woolworths beat third-quarter sales expectations on Thursday, driven by its core Australian Food business as customers stocked up on staples toward the end of the period.

The result builds on improving conditions flagged in February, when Woolworths said Australian Food sales accelerated early in the second half as value-focused shoppers split their spending across retailers, prompting the grocer to keep investing in lower prices, fresh ranges and loyalty rewards.

Australian Food, Woolworths' largest and most important profit engine, delivered sales of A$13.83 billion ($9.85 billion), up nearly 6% from a year earlier, with strong item growth the key driver.

The country's biggest grocer posted group sales of A$18.10 billion for the 13 weeks ended April 5, ahead of Visible Alpha consensus of A$17.98 billion and above A$17.31 billion reported a year earlier.

($1 = 1.4045 Australian dollars)

data.title: Soy basis steady-firmer; corn basis flat
data.description:
Spot basis bids for soybeans were steady to stronger in the U.S. Midwest on Wednesday, rising at two processing plants as soy crush margins marched higher, encouraging crushers to purchase fresh soybean supplies, spot checks showed.

  • The soybean basis improved by 5 cents a bushel at crushing sites in Lafayette, Indiana, and Sioux City, Iowa

  • Corn basis bids were steady and at least one broker noted an uptick in farmer selling as benchmark July Chicago Board of Trade corn futures (CN26) hit their highest in a month

  • Still, many growers remain focused on spring planting and fieldwork, limiting grain sales into country elevators

  • Elevators and processors have begun rolling their basis bids to the CBOT July corn, soybean and wheat futures contracts as the respective May futures contracts approach their May 14 expiration

  • The number of contracts registered for delivery against CBOT wheat futures rose to 434 contracts on Wednesday afternoon, up from just 34 lots a day earlier, CME Group data showed, indicating the likelihood of deliveries against CBOT May wheat futures on first notice day on Thursday

  • But delivery registrations for CBOT corn and soybean futures were unchanged on Wednesday afternoon

  • On the CBOT, July corn (CN26) settled up 2-1/4 cents at $4.77-3/4 a bushel after reaching $4.79, its highest since March 27, buoyed by strength in crude oil futures

  • CBOT July soybeans (SN26) closed up 7-3/4 cents at $11.97 a bushel while July wheat (WN26) ended down 4-3/4 cents at $6.53 a bushel

data.title: Independence Realty Trust, Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Independence Realty Trust, Inc reported quarterly breakeven results​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 4 cents. The mean expectation of six analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 3 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from zero cents to 8 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 2.5% to $165.33 million from a year ago; analysts expected $164.92 million.

  • Independence Realty Trust, Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was breakeven results​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $68 thousand.

  • Independence Realty Trust, Inc shares had risen by 9.6% this quarter and lost 6.6% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 5.9% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, one analyst negatively revised an earnings estimate

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 10 "strong buy" or "buy," 4 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the residential reits peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Independence Realty Trust, Inc is $18.50, about 11.9% above its last closing price of $16.29

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data April 29 at 11:12 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated.
